name: Photography & Video
description: Enquiry to delivered gallery for a photo or video studio, with a Shoots object holding the package, shoot date, location and delivery deadline.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for a photography and videography studio. A contact is the person booking and paying — a couple, a family, or a marketing or brand buyer commissioning commercial work — and for commercial jobs the person on set is often not the person who signed. A deal is one commission from enquiry through quote and retainer to the shoot and the delivered gallery. Shoot dates, call times, locations and delivery deadlines live on Shoot records; never invent a date, location, price, turnaround time or usage licence you were not given, and never promise availability you have not checked against the calendar.

automation_rules.name: Get pricing to a new enquiry within a day
automation_rules.description: Most photography enquiries book whoever replies first with a real price — a day's silence loses the date to someone else.

params.title: Reply to {{contact.first_name}} — check the date, send the pricing guide, offer two call slots
New shoot enquiry
params.body: Capture before quoting: shoot type, date and whether it is fixed, location, hours of coverage needed, how the images will be used (personal or commercial), and their budget range.

automation_rules.name: Keep a running history on every commission
automation_rules.description: Writes a timeline note whenever a booking moves stage, so the retainer, deliverables and turnaround promised are all in one place at edit time.

params.title: Booking stage updated: {{deal.title}}
params.body: Stage changed. Record what was agreed — shoot date and call time, location, package and hours, number of edited images, usage licence, and the delivery date promised.

kb_templates.company:
## About us

We are a photography and video studio based in [your city], shooting [your specialisms]. Replace this with your style in one honest sentence, how long you have been shooting, and the work you most want more of.

## Practicalities

- Studio address, or note that you shoot on location only
- Travel policy and mileage or day rate outside [radius]
- Public liability insurance and cover amount — venues and brands will ask for the certificate
- Backup policy: dual card slots, offsite copies, and how long you retain files
kb_templates.products:
## Packages

- **Mini session** — [n] minutes, [n] edited images, [price]
- **Half day** — [n] hours coverage, [n] edited images, [price]
- **Full day** — up to [n] hours, [n] edited images, [price]
- **Wedding collections** — [price] to [price], second shooter [price]
- **Commercial day rate** — [price], licence quoted separately

## Terms

Retainer of [amount or %] to hold a date, non-refundable. Balance due [n] days before the shoot. Turnaround: sneak peek in [n] days, full gallery in [n] weeks. Extra edits [price] each; rush delivery [price].
kb_templates.playbook:
## Qualifying an enquiry

1. Date and whether it is fixed — check the calendar before you reply with anything.
2. Shoot type, location, and hours of coverage actually needed.
3. How the images will be used. Personal versus commercial is the difference between a session fee and a licence.
4. Deliverables they expect — number of edited images, prints, album, raw files (say no to raws, and say why).
5. Budget range, before you build a quote.

## Consultation to booking

Show a full gallery from one similar shoot, not a highlight reel of twenty. Quote a package, not an hourly rate. Close on the retainer: the date is not held until it is paid.
kb_templates.process:
## From enquiry to delivered gallery

1. **Enquiry** — log the contact, date, shoot type and how they found you.
2. **Consultation Booked** — call or coffee; confirm style, coverage and expectations.
3. **Quote Sent** — package, deliverables, licence and turnaround in writing.
4. **Booked — Retainer Paid** — contract signed and retainer received; only now is the date held.
5. **Shoot Complete** — cards backed up twice before you leave the location.
6. **In Post-Production** — cull, edit, and send the sneak peek to keep them warm.
7. **Gallery Delivered** — balance settled, gallery sent, and ask for the review while they are still excited.
kb_templates.competitors:
## Why clients book us

Replace with your genuine differentiators. What actually decides it: a consistent style they can see across full galleries, a turnaround you have never missed, being easy to be around on the day, and proper insurance and backups.

## Common comparisons

- **Cheaper newer shooters** — real price pressure; compete on consistency and on delivering when you said you would, not on price.
- **Large studio chains** — brand reassurance, but the photographer is assigned and unknown until the day.
- **A guest with a good camera** — free, and the reason a lot of clients come back the following year for a reshoot.
